chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.S-472 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S). Hearing of case 1. For order on office objection No.1 to 5 a/w reply of counsel as at “A”. 2. For hearing of main case..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Dated 02.07.2025 Mr. Junaid Ahmed, advocate holds brief for Mr. Ali Abbas Chohan, Advocate for the petitioner. Petitioner is also present. Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Additional Prosecutor General. Mr. Shariq Mubashir, Assistant Advocate General Sindh. SIP Muhammad Younus Jutt, HC Imran, P.C Haroon Rasheed, P.S Landhi..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. On notices, SHO, P.S Landhi, respondent No.7, Additional SHO Younus Jutt, P.S Landhi, H.C Imran, P.S Landhi, respondent No.8, P.S Haroon Rasheed, P.S Landhi, respondent No.10 and Muhammad Owais Warsi, former SHO, P.S Landhi have filed their replies, which are taken on record wherein it has been submitted that on 12.05.2025 a complaint was filed by one Syed Shakeeluddin against the present petitioner alleging that he was harassing his son and pressurizing him to maintain sexual relations with him. On such complaint, the petitioner was called in Police Station and enquired. No harassment of any nature has been caused to him. The petitioner is present in person and submits that his counsel Mr. Ali Abbas Chohan is out of station. Since the petitioner had filed the instant petition seeking protection at the hands of the police officials which from the face of the material available on record appears to be a concocted story as the SHO is competent under the law to summon any person for the purposes of inquiry and the application filed by Shakeeluddin contains some sort of serious allegations against the petitioner.

Ali Abbas Chohan is out of station. Since the petitioner had filed the instant petition seeking protection at the hands of the police officials which from the face of the material available on record appears to be a concocted story as the SHO is competent under the law to summon any person for the purposes of inquiry and the application filed by Shakeeluddin contains some sort of serious allegations against the petitioner. It appears that the petitioner in order to save himself from the due course of law has filed this petition with malafide intention and ulterior motives. This petition is disposed of with direction to the police officials to conduct themselves in accordance with law and take action 2 against the petitioner if it is found that he is involved in any kind of criminal activity. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE Ayaz Gul
